management_notes:
Osisko Mining was led by John Burzynski (President & CEO) and the founding management team that built the original Osisko Mining Corporation — responsible for discovering and developing the Canadian Malartic gold mine, which was sold to Agnico Eagle and Yamana for approximately C$3.9 billion in 2014. The Windfall discovery and development represented Burzynski's second multi-billion-dollar exit in the same Abitibi greenstone belt within a decade. This is exceptional serial value creation by any measure.

Insider ownership at Osisko Mining was a standout feature. Management, founders, and insiders collectively held a significant equity stake consistent with a founder-led company, and the $1-per-share back-in right retained on the Windfall royalty stream was a deliberate alignment mechanism — management owned production economics as well as equity. The board included several members of the original Canadian Malartic team, providing continuity and deep institutional knowledge of the Quebec mining environment.

The Gold Fields acquisition at C$4.90/share (55% premium to the 20-day VWAP) received a 99.5% shareholder vote in favour — an extraordinary endorsement that reflects both the fair acquisition price and management's credibility with the retail and institutional shareholder base. Two C$2B+ exits in the same basin by the same team is a defining track record in the junior space. Score of 5.
geology_notes:
Windfall is one of the highest-grade gold deposits in Canada and globally competitive at the development stage. The January 2023 Feasibility Study (FS — the highest study tier) confirmed Probable Mineral Reserves of 3.159 million ounces at 8.06 g/t Au (12.16 Mt) — an exceptional grade for an underground bulk-mining operation at any scale. Measured and Indicated resources totalled approximately 4.05 million ounces at 11.4 g/t Au (inclusive of reserves), with Inferred resources adding further upside. This is a full NI 43-101 Proven and Probable Reserves declaration supported by a complete Feasibility Study.

The FS outlined an underground bulk-mining operation with average annual gold production of approximately 287,000 oz Au over a 10.6-year mine life. The project is located in the Urban-Barry area of Quebec's Abitibi Greenstone Belt — a Tier-1 global mining jurisdiction with established permitting, infrastructure, and workforce. The FS after-tax NPV(5%) was approximately C$1.97 billion at a US$1,700/oz gold base case; at the gold price prevailing at acquisition (~US$2,600/oz), the implied NPV was substantially higher, which is reflected in analyst consensus NAV estimates of C$4.50–C$5.00 per share.

The BELO permit (Bureau d'audiences publiques sur l'environnement — the key environmental authorisation) was received in October 2022, a major de-risking milestone that removed the primary permitting hurdle ahead of construction. Score of 5: Proven and Probable Reserves declared, full FS, Tier-1 Canadian jurisdiction, permitted.
capital_notes:
At time of acquisition (October 2024), Osisko Mining had approximately 441 million basic shares outstanding on a fully diluted basis. The share count was elevated relative to the early-stage foundation of the company due to a series of equity financings required to fund the extensive 1.6-million-metre drill program at Windfall over a decade. Warrant and option overhang existed but was being absorbed over time as the acquisition process advanced.

The balance sheet position was adequate but not exceptional for a development-stage company facing a C$1.35 billion construction capex (per the 2023 FS update). Osisko had secured C$300M in project financing commitments (from Orion Mine Finance and others) and the Osisko Gold Royalties streaming arrangement provided additional pre-production capital, but the company would have required additional capital markets activity to fully fund construction. The diluted share count and ongoing capital needs are the factors preventing a maximum capital score.

The acquisition by Gold Fields eliminated the financing overhang entirely. From a pre-acquisition scorecard standpoint, the capital structure was adequate — not pristine — for a development-stage company with a world-class asset. Score of 4: strong enough to reach the finish line with the right partner, but not a standalone clean structure.
catalyst_notes:
The catalyst score of 5 reflects the retrospective reality that the ultimate value-crystallising event — a C$4.90/share, 55% premium, all-cash acquisition by Gold Fields (the world's seventh-largest gold producer) for C$2.16 billion total — was completed on October 25, 2024. This is the single most powerful catalyst a junior mining company can achieve: a completed, fully valued acquisition at a premium to NAV by a major gold producer.

Pre-acquisition, the catalyst stack was exceptionally strong: the BELO permit had been received (October 2022), a full Feasibility Study was published (January 2023), and the company was advancing detailed engineering and construction mobilisation. Construction decision was effectively made pending financing, with the Gold Fields acquisition resolving the financing question entirely. The acquisition announcement (April 2024) followed by the vote (September 2024) and close (October 2024) provided three consecutive positive catalysts within a seven-month window.

For the purposes of the retrospective scorecard, a completed premium acquisition to a major represents the ideal outcome — maximum value crystallisation for all shareholders who held to close. Score of 5.
acquisition_notes:
Gold Fields acquired Osisko Mining at C$4.90 per share (all-cash), representing a 55% premium to the 20-day VWAP prior to announcement. Total consideration was approximately C$2.16 billion (US$1.6 billion). Sell-side analyst consensus NAV at the time of acquisition was approximately C$4.50–C$5.00 per share, implying the acquisition was done at approximately 1.0–1.09x analyst-consensus NAV — a full and fair price by acquisition standards for a permitted, FS-complete gold development project.

The FS after-tax NPV(5%) of approximately C$1.97 billion at US$1,700/oz gold translates to approximately C$4.47/diluted share (C$1.97B ÷ ~441M diluted shares). At the spot gold price prevailing at acquisition (~US$2,600–2,800/oz), the updated NPV would be meaningfully higher — potentially C$6.00–7.00 per share using linear gold price sensitivity. The acquisition at C$4.90 therefore represented a modest discount to an updated spot-price NAV but a full-price transaction relative to consensus. No distressed sale dynamics were present — the 99.5% shareholder vote confirms broad investor acceptance.

This is the benchmark outcome for the Verdict Framework: a FS-complete, permitted, high-grade underground gold project in a Tier-1 jurisdiction, acquired at a premium by a senior producer. Score of 5.
analyst_summary:
NOTE: OSK (Osisko Mining Corporation) was delisted from the TSX on October 25, 2024, following a completed all-cash acquisition by Gold Fields Limited (JSE/NYSE: GFI) at C$4.90 per share, for a total equity consideration of approximately C$2.16 billion (US$1.6 billion). The TSX ticker OSK is no longer active. Any exposure to the Windfall asset is now through Gold Fields. This scorecard is a retrospective/archival assessment. Confidence: high.

Osisko Mining was the exemplary outcome under the Verdict Framework: a world-class, high-grade underground gold deposit (3.159 Moz Probable Reserves @ 8.06 g/t Au per the January 2023 Feasibility Study) in Tier-1 Quebec, permitted (BELO October 2022), built by a serial value-creation management team (John Burzynski's second C$2B+ exit in the Abitibi belt), and ultimately acquired at a 55% premium by a major global gold producer. Composite score: 24/25 — BUY.

The acquisition at C$4.90/share was essentially at analyst-consensus NAV (~C$4.50–5.00/share) and at approximately 1.0x FS NAV per diluted share (C$1.97B NPV5 at US$1,700/oz ÷ ~441M diluted shares ≈ C$4.47). Shareholders who held from the announcement to close captured the full 55% premium. The 99.5% shareholder vote in favour reflects the quality of management's execution and the fairness of the Gold Fields offer. The single point withheld (capital score of 4 rather than 5) reflects the elevated diluted share count and construction financing requirements that made an acquisition partner necessary. This is the standard against which all other junior developers in the framework should be measured.
